landing.whatsappMessage: Hi! I came from the $t(brand.name) website and would like to learn more.
landing.heroSubtitle: From inventory control to an exclusive Client Portal. The complete system for builders who want to scale without losing control.
landing.painTitle: Your construction project doesn't have to be a
landing.painSubtitle: No more stress. See how $t(brand.name) transforms your daily routine.
landing.pain1: Inventory shortages and expensive emergency purchases
landing.solution1: Precise tracking of material ins and outs
landing.pain2: Pay upfront, lose track of advances, and the contractor walks away?
landing.solution2: Progress-Based Billing. The system calculates completion, deducts advances automatically, and applies Technical Retention before a single dollar leaves your pocket.
landing.pain3: Client demanding deadlines and you're lost
landing.solution3: Visual schedule and automated Daily Construction Report
landing.pain4: Notes on scraps of paper that disappear
landing.solution4: Everything in the cloud, accessible from anywhere
landing.demoSubtitle: A quick 2-minute tour showing the main features
landing.stepsSubtitle: No complexity. No training. Just start.
landing.step1Desc: Define your project phases — we have ready-made templates!
landing.step2Desc: Add materials and manage ins/outs in 2 clicks.
landing.step3Desc: Export professional PDFs and impress your client.
landing.financialSubtitle: Professionalize your relationship with contractors and avoid overpaying.
landing.finCard1Text: Enter the % completed, the app tells you the exact net amount to pay.
landing.finCard2Text: The system automatically deducts any pending advance at billing time.
landing.finCard3Text: We hold 5% (or whatever you set) of each payment as a delivery guarantee.
landing.finCard4Text: Generate detailed financial reports by phase with one click.
landing.feat1Desc: Control multiple job sites on the same screen.
landing.feat2Desc: The app warns you when cement is running low.
landing.feat3Desc: Your site foreman logs the daily report, you approve it.
landing.feat4Desc: Track Planned vs. Actual for each phase. Retention balance and total paid in real time.
landing.businessTitle: Why Growing Construction Companies use the
landing.businessSubtitle: Exclusive features for those who want to scale without losing control.
landing.biz1Title: The End of "How's the project going?"
landing.biz1Desc: Deliver an exclusive Client Portal. Your client follows photos and progress on their phone, without seeing costs. You gain trust and peace of mind.
landing.biz2Desc: Got 5 projects? The system unifies material lists from all of them. Quote 500 bags of cement at once and increase your profit margin.
landing.biz3Desc: Profitability Dashboard. Know exactly which project is making money and which is draining your cash.
landing.pricingTitle: How much does it cost to
landing.pricingSubtitle: Every untracked advance, every guessed billing — it's money out of your pocket. Choose the plan that protects your cash.
landing.plan2Desc: For those who want to ditch paper and job-site chaos.
landing.plan2Feat3: 🧱 Inventory Control: Know what's on site.
landing.plan3Desc: Stop losing money on contractors and materials.
landing.plan3Feat2: 💰 Progress Billing: Pay only what's done.
landing.plan4Desc: Scale your construction company and impress your clients.
landing.plan4Feat2: 📱 Client Portal: Public link for homeowners to follow progress.
landing.plan4Feat3: 🛒 Purchasing Module: Unified lists for quoting.
landing.testimonialsSubtitle: Real results from builders and contractors who have already transformed their project management.
landing.test1Content: I used to lose 20% of materials on every project. With $t(brand.name), waste dropped to zero. That alone paid for the subscription — and then some.
landing.test2Content: The daily PDF report protected me during a labor dispute. Incredibly professional. My clients actually call to compliment how organized everything is.
landing.test3Content: I kept overpaying subcontractors without realizing it. With $t(brand.name)'s billing system, advance deductions are automatic. Saved over USD 1,200 just last month.
landing.test4Content: I never thought I'd use an app on a job site. $t(brand.name) changed everything. I track lumber and concrete on the spot without calling the office. No more work stoppages.
landing.faq1A: Yes! The app works offline and syncs automatically when you reconnect. Perfect for job sites without Wi-Fi.
landing.faq2A: No! $t(brand.name) was built to be used one-handed, right on the job site. Big buttons, simple interface, no hassle.
landing.faq3Q: Does it work for small renovations?
landing.faq3A: Yes, any type of project! From a bathroom renovation to a high-end condo. The system adapts to your project.
landing.faq4Q: How does the billing and technical retention system work?
landing.faq4A: You set the labor contract value per phase or item in the schedule. At each billing, enter the percentage completed and the system automatically calculates the gross value, deducts pending advances, and applies technical retention (default 5%, but you can customize). The result is the exact net amount to pay, no surprises.
landing.faq5Q: Do I need to install any software?
landing.faq5A: No! $t(brand.name) runs directly in your phone or computer browser. Just access, create your account, and start.
landing.faq6A: Yes, no red tape. You can cancel your subscription whenever you want, with no cancellation fee.
landing.ctaTitle: Take full control of your inventory and your money
landing.ctaSubtitle: No more spreadsheets, lost papers, and cash leaks. Start now, it's free.

adminAccess.title: How to access your account (Administrator)
adminAccess.step1: Open the app on the Home Screen.
adminAccess.step2: Enter your registered Email and Password.
adminAccess.note: If you forgot your password, use the "Forgot my password" link below the password field.

pinAccess.title: How to access as a Team Guest (PIN Access)
pinAccess.step1: Click the invite link you received via WhatsApp.
pinAccess.step2: On the Guest Access screen, enter the numeric PIN provided by the administrator.
pinAccess.step3: The system will automatically validate the code.
pinAccess.step4: Fill in your name, email and create a personal password for future access.
pinAccess.step5: Done! You're now inside the project.

createWork.title: How to register your first project and define phases
createWork.step1: On the Dashboard, tap the "+" button (bottom right corner) to open the "New Project" form.
createWork.step2: Add a photo of the lot or project by tapping "Tap to add photo".
createWork.step3: Fill in the "Project name" field (e.g., Residential House Silva).
createWork.step4: Fill in the "Address" field (Street, number, neighborhood).
createWork.step5: Tap the "Create Project" button to save.
createWork.step6: The system will automatically create 6 default phases for your project: Preliminary Services, Foundation, Structure, Roofing, Installations, and Finishing.
createWork.step7: Go to the "Schedule" tab inside the project to view and manage all phases and their items.
createWork.note: Each phase comes with pre-registered checklist items. You can track progress for each item as the project advances.

addMaterial.title: How to add materials and manage your inventory
addMaterial.step1: Inside the project, tap the "Inventory" tab in the top menu.
addMaterial.step2: Tap the orange "+ Add Material" button to open the registration form.
addMaterial.step3: In the "Filter by phase" field, select the construction phase to see suggested materials (e.g., Foundation, Structure). Or leave it on "All phases".
addMaterial.step4: In the "Material name" field, select a material from the pre-registered list or type a custom name. Suggested materials auto-fill the unit of measurement.
addMaterial.step5: Choose the "Unit" of measurement (m³, kg, un, etc.), enter the "Current qty" in stock and the "Minimum qty" for reorder alerts.
addMaterial.step6: Tap the "Add" button to save the material to your inventory.
addMaterial.step7: The registered material will appear in the inventory list. Use the "+" and "−" buttons to quickly log entries and exits.
addMaterial.note: The system includes a smart list of pre-registered materials organized by phase. When you select a suggested material, the unit of measurement is auto-filled, speeding up the registration process.

financialMeasurements.title: How to create Measurements, deduct Advances and apply Technical Retention
financialMeasurements.step1: Go to the "Schedule" tab and set the labor contract value for each item. Click the "$" icon next to the item or use the "Set Total" button on the phase bar to distribute the value automatically among items.
financialMeasurements.step2: When distributing by phase, choose the mode: "Equal among items" (equal division) or "Proportional" (based on each item's weight). Click "Distribute among items" to confirm.
financialMeasurements.step3: Go to the "Financial" tab to view the full dashboard: Completed Value, Total Paid, Retention Balance and Pending Advances.
financialMeasurements.step4: To register an advance (voucher), click "New Advance". Fill in the Date, Amount and a Description (e.g., Lunch voucher). Click "Register Advance" to save.
financialMeasurements.step5: The registered advance will appear in the "Advances (Vouchers)" section with "Pending" status until it is deducted in a future measurement.
financialMeasurements.step6: To create a new measurement, click the orange "New Measurement" button. The system will open a 4-step wizard: Selection → Calculation → Deductions → Result.
financialMeasurements.step7: In the "Selection" step, set the measurement date and select the schedule items that have a defined contract value. Only items with a value will appear in the list.
financialMeasurements.step8: In the "Deductions" step, the system automatically calculates Technical Retention (default 5%, editable per project) and shows pending advances available for deduction. Select which advances to offset in this measurement.
financialMeasurements.step9: In the "Result" step, review the summary: Gross Measured Value, Technical Retention, Deducted Advances and Net Amount Payable. Confirm to register the measurement.
financialMeasurements.step10: To export a complete financial statement as PDF, click the document icon (📄) next to the "New Advance" button. The PDF includes the phase comparison (Contracted vs. Completed vs. Paid), measurement history and advances.
financialMeasurements.note: The financial dashboard displays a "Phase Comparison" chart with colored bars: gray (Contracted), orange (Completed) and green (Paid). If the completed value exceeds the contract, the system will issue a contract overrun alert to the administrator.

clientPortal.title: How to share the Client Portal and impress buyers
clientPortal.step1: Open the desired project and tap the menu icon (⋮) in the top right corner. A panel with owner options will appear.
clientPortal.step2: In the menu, select "Client Portal" (share icon). The portal configuration modal will open.
clientPortal.step3: Toggle the "Active Portal" switch to automatically generate a unique tracking link. The message will change to "Your client can access the link".
clientPortal.step4: The "Portal Link" field will display the unique URL (e.g., https://obracerta.pro/portal/...). Use the copy button (📋) to copy the link or the external link button (🔗) to open the portal in a new tab.
clientPortal.step5: Send the copied link directly via WhatsApp to the homeowner. They can track the construction progress in real time without creating an account.
clientPortal.step6: The client will see on the portal: the project name, address, overall progress (%), the "Construction Stages" timeline with the status of each phase (Completed, Pending) and the "Photo Gallery" with all images from the Construction Diary.
clientPortal.step7: To revoke access at any time, go back to the menu (⋮) > "Client Portal" and turn off the "Active Portal" switch. The link will be immediately invalidated.
clientPortal.note: Privacy guaranteed: the client does NOT see any financial data — no contract values, measurements, advances or retentions. They only see photos, progress and stages. It's the perfect way to keep buyers engaged and confident in your project.

teamInvite.title: How to invite your team and set access permissions
teamInvite.step1: Open the desired project and tap the menu icon (⋮) in the top right corner. In the options panel, select "Manage Access".
teamInvite.step2: The "Manage Access" modal will open showing the project name, your current plan (e.g., Business) and the number of users in use (e.g., 1/5 users).
teamInvite.step3: In the "Create New Invite" section, select the desired permission level from the dropdown: "User" (restricted access, cannot see financials) or "Admin" (full access, including financials and editing).
teamInvite.step4: Click the orange "+ Generate PIN" button. The system will automatically create a 6-digit PIN and display a notification "Invite created! PIN: XXXXXX – Share with the user".
teamInvite.step5: The invite will appear in the "Pending Invites" section with the permission badge (Admin/User), a countdown timer (e.g., "Expires in 7 days") and two sharing buttons.
teamInvite.step6: Use the "Copy Link" button to copy the magic invite link or the green "WhatsApp" button to directly send a personalized message with the project name and access link.
teamInvite.step7: In the "Users with Access" section, you'll see all members linked to the project. You can change each user's permission (Admin ↔ User) via the dropdown next to their name, or remove them by clicking the trash icon (🗑️).
teamInvite.step8: Unused invites can be deleted at any time using the trash icon next to the pending invite. Expired invites stop working automatically after 7 days.
teamInvite.note: Data security: users with "User" permission do NOT have access to the Financial tab, measurements, advances or contract values. Only members with "Admin" permission can view and edit financial data. This ensures that foremen and assistants can follow the schedule and stock without accessing sensitive information.

dailyLog.title: How to fill out the Construction Diary and generate PDF Reports
dailyLog.step1: Open the desired project on the Dashboard and click the "Diary" tab in the top menu. The screen will automatically display "Today's Record" with the current date.
dailyLog.step2: Select the day's weather from the 4 available options: Sunny, Partly Cloudy, Cloudy or Rainy. This information will be used in report statistics.
dailyLog.step3: In the "What was done today?" field, the system automatically fills in completed activities from the Schedule and Stock movements. An orange notice says: "Content pre-filled automatically. You can edit freely". Adjust as needed.
dailyLog.step4: Use the "Observations (optional)" field to record problems, pending items, materials used or any important site notes.
dailyLog.step5: In the "Workers on Site" section, select the role from the dropdown (e.g., Mason, Helper, Electrician), set the quantity and click the "+" button to add each professional present.
dailyLog.step6: In the "Machinery and Equipment" section, select the equipment from the dropdown (e.g., Concrete Mixer, Scaffolding, Backhoe), set the quantity and click "+" to log the machinery used.
dailyLog.step7: In "Construction Photos", use "Take Photo" to capture images directly from the camera or "Gallery" to select existing photos. Photos are automatically compressed. Tap the edit icon (✏️) to add captions.
dailyLog.step8: Click the orange "Save Report" button to store the daily record. After saving, the record will appear in the history with a weather icon and activity summary.
dailyLog.step9: To generate consolidated reports, use the "Weekly" and "Monthly" buttons in the top right corner. The Weekly Report shows days worked, attendance and daily activities. The Monthly Report offers "Summary" and "Charts" tabs with productivity dashboards. Both have an "Export PDF" button.
dailyLog.step10: In the "Settings" menu (⚙️ icon), access "Configure PIN" to create a 4-6 digit PIN that protects edits to saved records. In "Configure Company", upload your logo (PNG/JPG up to 5MB) and company name to customize the header of all exported PDFs.
dailyLog.note: Smart automation: the Construction Diary cross-references data from the Schedule and Stock to pre-fill your daily activities. This saves time and ensures no important information is missed. All records create an auditable history — edits to saved reports require a PIN and justification, ensuring your project's document integrity.

profitDashboard.title: How to analyze the Profitability Dashboard and your projects' ROI
profitDashboard.step1: On the main Dashboard, find the top icon menu. Click the second icon — the ascending line chart (📈) — to access the "Profitability Dashboard". This module is exclusive to the Business plan.
profitDashboard.step2: At the top of the screen, you'll find 3 strategic cards that automatically consolidate data from all your projects: "Total Contracted" (sum of all contract values), "Total Profit" (revenue minus material and labor costs) and "Overall ROI" (percentage return on investment).
profitDashboard.step3: Below the cards, the "Profit by Project" chart displays comparative bars showing each project's profit in monetary values. Hover over the bars to see exact details for each project.
profitDashboard.step4: In the "Breakdown by Project" section, each project is listed individually with three indicators: Contract (total contract value), Costs (sum of consumed materials and labor paid via measurements) and Profit (difference between contract and costs). A colored badge on the right shows each project's ROI%.
profitDashboard.step5: Data is automatically calculated by the system based on contract values defined in the Schedule, unit prices from Stock and payments recorded in Measurements. No manual data entry is required on this screen.
profitDashboard.step6: Use the ROI to quickly identify which projects are most profitable and which need attention. An ROI above 30% indicates good margins; below 10% may signal a need to review costs or renegotiate the contract.
profitDashboard.step7: To return to the main Dashboard, click the "←" arrow in the top left corner or use the "Dashboard" breadcrumb in the top navigation bar.
profitDashboard.note: Automatic business intelligence: the Profitability Dashboard cross-references contracts, measurements and stock costs to calculate your real profit in real time. The more data you feed into the Schedule, Stock and Financial modules, the more accurate your strategic overview becomes. This module is exclusive to the Business plan.

purchasesAlerts.title: How to use the Purchases Module and Stock Alerts
purchasesAlerts.step1: On the main Dashboard, find the top icon menu. Click the first icon — the shopping cart (🛒) — to access the "Purchases Module". Alternatively, click the alert icon with the red badge (🔔) to go directly to "Stock Alerts". Both modules are exclusive to the Business plan.
purchasesAlerts.step2: In the "Purchases Module", the system automatically scans all your projects and lists only materials with stock below the configured minimum. At the top, an orange banner shows the total: e.g., "3 items below minimum — Across all your projects".
purchasesAlerts.step3: Each material displays the name, source project and "Needs: X un" highlighted in red. This value is calculated automatically: Needs = Minimum Quantity − Current Quantity. Next to it, you can check "Current: X / Min: Y" for full context.
purchasesAlerts.step4: Use the "Search material or category..." bar to quickly find a specific item. Materials are grouped by category (e.g., Uncategorized, Foundation, Finishing), making it easy to organize your quotation list.
purchasesAlerts.step5: In the top right corner, click the orange "Export PDF" button to generate a professional quotation list with all missing materials. The PDF includes the company name, date, and exact quantities to purchase — ready to send to suppliers via WhatsApp or email.
purchasesAlerts.step6: On the "Stock Alerts" screen (accessible via the alert icon 🔔), you'll find two cards at the top: "Materials on alert" (total critical items) and "Orders placed" (items already marked as requested from suppliers).
purchasesAlerts.step7: Use the "All projects" filter in the top right to view alerts for a specific project or all at once. Each material displays a red progress bar indicating the critical level and a badge with the deficit (e.g., -6 bags, -0.50 kg).
purchasesAlerts.step8: To record that a material has been requested from a supplier, click the "Order" button next to the item. The system updates the "Orders placed" counter and visually highlights the item with a blue border and "Ordered" badge in the Stock module, preventing duplicate purchases.
purchasesAlerts.note: Smart prevention: the Purchases Module and Stock Alerts work together to ensure your project never stops due to material shortage. Keep the "Minimum Quantity" and "Unit Price" fields updated for each material in Stock — this automatically feeds deficit calculations and quotation reports. The more accurate the records, the more reliable your shopping list.
